findings.message: Mutation accepts 'description' input parameter but does not use it in the resolver implementation. The parameter is silently discarded, creating a data contract mismatch that will confuse clients and lead to unexpected behavior.
findings.suggestion: Either (1) pass the 'description' parameter to repository.create_item() and update the repository function to accept and store it, or (2) remove 'description' from the mutation signature if it is not intended to be supported.

findings.message: Mutation returns a bare Item scalar instead of a typed mutation payload object. Without a payload wrapper, the mutation cannot return success/error status or validation errors to clients.
findings.suggestion: Create a CreateItemPayload type with success/errors fields and return it from the mutation, allowing proper error handling and client feedback.
